title: ndd Medical EasyOne Series Pulmonary Function Tester - Flow Sensor / Ultrasonic Measurement Errors
description: Guide to troubleshooting ultrasonic flow measurement errors caused by contamination, setup issues, environmental conditions, or sensor faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively in use on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Error Message and Repeatability
steps.instructions: Note the exact error message related to flow or ultrasonic measurement. Attempt a new test or calibration to confirm if the issue repeats.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Tube / Patient Interface
steps.instructions: Check for moisture, debris, residue, or damage. Replace with a known-good flow tube if issues are found.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Flow Tube Type and Orientation
steps.instructions: Ensure correct flow tube is installed, oriented properly, and fully seated with no looseness.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Evaluate for airflow, temperature, and humidity issues. Move device to a stable environment if needed.

steps.title: Verify Calibration / Verification Check (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Perform calibration or verification using a known good syringe if supported.

steps.title: Inspect Device Ports and Air Path
steps.instructions: Check for dust or obstruction and clean externally per protocol without disassembly.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn device off, wait 30 seconds, and power back on.

CCR.Complaint: Spirometer reporting inconsistent or inaccurate flow readings during patient testing.
CCR.Cause: Contaminated flow tube causing disruption of ultrasonic measurement.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced flow tube and verified normal operation with repeat testing.

helpfulDetails: Environmental conditions at time of issue

title: ndd Medical EasyOne Series Pulmonary Function Tester - Wireless / Bluetooth Connectivity Failure
description: Troubleshooting wireless and Bluetooth connectivity issues including pairing failures, interference, and configuration problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively being used on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Reported Issue and Device Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact issue (unable to pair, intermittent connection, or device not visible) and attempt to reconnect to confirm repeatability.

steps.title: Confirm Bluetooth / Wireless is Enabled on Device
steps.instructions: Ensure Bluetooth or wireless is turned ON and the device is in pairing/discoverable mode.

steps.title: Verify Receiving Device (PC / Tablet) Settings
steps.instructions: Check that Bluetooth is enabled, the correct device is selected, and no airplane mode or restrictions are active.

steps.title: Remove and Re-Pair Device
steps.instructions: Delete the device from paired list, restart both systems, and perform a fresh pairing process.

steps.title: Check for Interference or Range Issues
steps.instructions: Ensure devices are within range and minimize interference from other Bluetooth or Wi-Fi devices.

steps.title: Inspect for Multiple Device Conflicts
steps.instructions: Verify the device is not connected to another system and disconnect if necessary.

steps.title: Verify Software / Application Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the application is running, properly configured, and has Bluetooth permissions. Restart if needed.

steps.title: Check Battery Level and Power Stability
steps.instructions: Confirm adequate battery level as low power can affect wireless performance.

steps.title: Perform Full Device Restart
steps.instructions: Power cycle both the spirometer and receiving device to clear temporary issues.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported EasyOne spirometer would not connect to tablet via Bluetooth.
CCR.Cause: Corrupt Bluetooth pairing and device previously connected to another workstation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed old pairing, restarted devices, and successfully re-paired spirometer to tablet.

helpfulDetails: Battery level at time of issue

title: ndd Medical EasyOne Series Pulmonary Function Tester - Battery not charging or power issues
description: Identifies causes of battery not charging, device not powering on, or intermittent power loss due to external power, charger, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not in use on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Power Symptoms
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the device will not power on, battery is not charging, or intermittent shutdown occurs. Attempt to power on with and without charger connected.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify outlet functionality using a known working device and ensure any power strip is ON.

steps.title: Inspect Power Adapter and Cable
steps.instructions: Check for loose connections, damaged cable, or worn connector and confirm use of correct manufacturer-approved adapter.

steps.title: Confirm Charging Indicators
steps.instructions: Observe for charging light or icon and gently move connector to check for intermittent contact.

steps.title: Attempt Power-On While Plugged In
steps.instructions: With charger connected, attempt to power on. If it powers on, suspect battery degradation. If not, suspect charger or internal power issue.

steps.title: Allow Charging Time and Recheck
steps.instructions: Leave device connected for 15–30 minutes and attempt power on again.

steps.title: Check Environmental or Usage Factors
steps.instructions: Confirm no extreme temperatures, clean/dry contacts, and no liquid exposure.

steps.title: Perform Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: If device powers intermittently, power off completely and restart after a brief pause.

CCR.Complaint: Device reported not charging and unable to power on during use.
CCR.Cause: Failed battery or charging circuit suspected after verifying outlet, adapter, and cable were functional.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent to Clinical Engineering for further evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Device powers on with AC only vs not at all
Condition of charging port and cable
Any signs of heat, damage, or liquid exposure

title: ndd Medical EasyOne Series Pulmonary Function Tester - Inaccurate FVC / FEV1 Readings
description: Identifies causes of inaccurate FVC/FEV1 values due to technique, contamination, calibration issues, or environmental factors affecting ultrasonic measurement.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively being used on a patient. Move testing to another spirometer if needed.

steps.title: Verify Reported Inaccuracy
steps.instructions: Confirm what is incorrect (FVC or FEV1 lower or higher than expected, inconsistent results between tests). Repeat a test using a simulator or known subject if available.

steps.title: Check Patient Technique (If Reported During Use)
steps.instructions: Confirm proper testing technique including tight seal around mouthpiece, no air leaks, proper nose clip use, and full inhalation with forceful exhalation.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Tube / Mouthpiece
steps.instructions: Check for moisture, debris, or damage and replace with a new or known-good flow tube or mouthpiece.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Evaluate for airflow disturbances or extreme temperature/humidity and relocate device if needed.

steps.title: Perform Device Calibration Verification (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Verify calibration requirements and perform a calibration check using a known syringe if supported.

steps.title: Verify Device Settings and Patient Profile
steps.instructions: Ensure correct patient demographics and correct test type are selected.

steps.title: Restart Device
steps.instructions: Power cycle the device completely.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported inconsistent and inaccurate FVC/FEV1 readings compared to previous patient baselines.
CCR.Cause: Moisture and residue contamination found in the flow tube affecting ultrasonic measurement accuracy.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced flow tube and verified accurate readings with repeat testing.

title: ndd Medical EasyOne Series Pulmonary Function Tester - Software freeze or data sync errors
description: Troubleshooting freezing, unresponsive software, and data synchronization issues caused by storage, connectivity, or system conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not actively being used on a patient and ensure all completed tests are saved locally before proceeding.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Behavior
steps.instructions: Determine if the issue is a complete freeze, slow response, or sync failure and attempt to reproduce the issue.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart
steps.instructions: Power cycle the device and restart any connected PC or tablet.

steps.title: Check Device Storage Capacity
steps.instructions: Review storage and delete old or transferred test data if near capacity.

steps.title: Verify Connectivity (USB / Bluetooth / Wireless)
steps.instructions: Check cables, confirm wireless connection, ensure correct pairing, and reconnect if needed.

steps.title: Confirm Receiving System Status (PC / EMR / App)
steps.instructions: Ensure receiving system is responsive, correct session is selected, and restart application if needed.

steps.title: Check Software Version and Updates
steps.instructions: Verify device and associated software are up to date.

steps.title: Inspect for Environmental or Usage Factors
steps.instructions: Check for overheating, long uptime, or interference and allow device to cool or relocate if needed.

CCR.Complaint: Device freezing during testing and unable to sync results to EMR.
CCR.Cause: Device storage near capacity and intermittent Bluetooth connection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared stored data, re-paired device, and restored normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or removed)
